data.title: JPMorgan: Ethereum upgrade failed to effectively boost network activity
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) said in a research report that despite the continuous upgrades of the Ethereum blockchain, its activity has not increased significantly. The bank noted that since the Dencun upgrade in March 2024, average and total fees have declined, and neither the number of daily transactions nor the number of active addresses has seen substantial growth. Additionally, futures positions suggest that institutions have played an important role in the recent rise in ETH. Ethereum launched the Pectra upgrade on May 7, an update designed to simplify the staking process, enhance wallet functionality, and improve overall efficiency. . (CoinDesk)

data.title: Ethereum Activity Remains Stagnant Despite Recent Upgrades
data.description: According to Odaily, JPMorgan has released a research report indicating that despite ongoing upgrades to the Ethereum blockchain, its activity levels have not significantly increased. The bank highlighted that since the Dencun upgrade in March 2024, both average and total fees have decreased, yet there has been no substantial growth in daily transaction numbers or active addresses. Additionally, futures positions suggest that institutional investors have played a significant role in the recent rise of ETH. On May 7, Ethereum initiated the Pectra upgrade, aimed at simplifying the staking process, enhancing wallet functionality, and improving overall efficiency.

data.title: Today, the US Bitcoin ETF has a net outflow of 1,977 BTC, and the Ethereum ETF has a net inflow of 37,685 ETH
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Lookonchain monitoring, today 10 US Bitcoin ETFs had a net outflow of 1,977 BTC, of ​​which Fidelity had an outflow of 1,571 BTC. Fidelity currently holds 198,291 BTC, worth US$20.95 billion; 9 Ethereum ETFs had a net inflow of 37,685 ETH, of which BlackRock had an inflow of 19,069 ETH. BlackRock currently holds 1,389,780 ETH, worth US$3.59 billion.

data.title: Stablecoin Growth Drives Demand for U.S. Short-Term Treasury Bonds
data.description:
According to Foresight News, a recent report by CoinDesk highlights that Citigroup has observed an increasing demand for U.S. short-term Treasury bonds, driven by the growing use of stablecoins in both the crypto market and traditional finance.

The report underscores the dominance of dollar-backed stablecoins, such as USDT, in crypto trading and blockchain payments, reflecting the U.S. dollar's status as a global reserve currency. Legislation under consideration by Congress could further solidify this trend by requiring reserves to hold short-term government debt. Additionally, traditional financial giants like PayPal and Visa are exploring stablecoin applications.

Citigroup forecasts that the stablecoin market could expand to between $1.6 trillion and $3.7 trillion by 2030.

data.title: The final value of the US one-year inflation rate in May is 6.6%, which is expected to be 7.1%
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The final value of the expected inflation rate for the United States in May is 6.6%, the expected value is 7.1%, and the previous value is 7.30%.
The final value of the expected inflation rate for the United States in May for the five to ten years is 4.2%, the expected value is 4.5%, and the previous value is 4.60%. (Jinshi)

data.title: SEC Dismisses Lawsuit Against Binance, Signaling Shift in U.S. Crypto Policy
data.description:
Binance Blog published a new article, highlighting the significant development of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) dismissing its lawsuit against Binance. This decision marks a pivotal shift in the U.S. approach to cryptocurrency, signaling a global endorsement for responsible crypto innovation. The dismissal not only boosts confidence in the digital finance sector from the U.S. to the EU but also validates Binance's compliance record, prompting a reevaluation of leadership in digital finance.

The SEC's decision is a landmark moment for Binance, the cryptocurrency industry, and the U.S. The closure of this long-standing legal case opens a new chapter, indicating that the U.S. is once again prioritizing innovation. This message resonates with entrepreneurs in Silicon Valley and Austin, developers in Nairobi, regulators in Brussels, and users worldwide. The decision benefits U.S. consumers, the economy, and innovators shaping the future of finance, while also having a positive ripple effect on global users by legitimizing and clarifying the digital asset space. Historically, the U.S. has been a central force in global finance, and its previous approach to crypto often created uncertainty due to a lack of clear legislative frameworks. However, with bipartisan crypto bills advancing and regulatory practices being reconsidered, the dismissal of the lawsuit signals a shift from enforcement to clarity.

The courage to correct course is acknowledged, with gratitude extended to Chairman Paul Atkins and the Trump administration for supporting progress. U.S. President Donald Trump and his administration recognized the need for rules that facilitate responsible innovation rather than obstruct it. The dismissal of the SEC lawsuit is not just about Binance; it reflects the company's commitment to building a world-class compliance program that aligns with the highest global standards. Despite not serving U.S. users, Binance respects and adheres to U.S. regulatory expectations, understanding the global influence of U.S. standards. Currently, Binance holds 21 regulatory approvals and licenses worldwide, including in G7 nations with stringent AML regulations, more than any other global exchange. With a compliance team of over 650 members and a top-tier financial crimes unit, Binance actively supports law enforcement in combating crypto-related crime globally.

For the United States, this moment is an opportunity to reclaim its leadership role in digital finance. Global regulators often look to Washington for guidance, and this dismissal may encourage a more open approach to digital assets. With Binance's compliance program validated, the default posture of suspicion is challenged, paving the way for broader adoption, secure access, and clearer pathways for innovators. The dismissal also strengthens the case for balanced, collaborative regulation, allowing the global conversation to focus on solutions that protect users without hindering progress. For users, this development restores confidence in Binance and the legitimacy of crypto, demonstrating that global exchanges can be both regulated and responsible. This moment is a testament to Binance's commitment to responsible innovation, ensuring user safety, regulatory compliance, and the exclusion of bad actors. While the dismissal is a significant milestone, Binance remains dedicated to advancing the future of crypto, both in the U.S. and globally.

data.title: U.S. Inflation Rate Expectations for May Show Decline
data.description: According to Odaily, the final expected one-year inflation rate in the United States for May is 6.6%, which is lower than the anticipated 7.1% and the previous value of 7.30%. Additionally, the final expected inflation rate for the five to ten-year period is 4.2%, below the forecasted 4.5% and the prior figure of 4.60%.

data.title: SlowMist Cosine: Cetus hackers have not yet transferred more than $60 million worth of ETH
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: SlowMist Yuxian posted on the X platform that the Cetus hacker has not returned the funds, and the ETH worth more than 60 million US dollars has not been transferred. At present, it has received clues about the suspected Cetus hacker submitted by community users, and more details may be disclosed later.

data.title: Cetus Hacker Yet to Return Stolen Funds, Community Submits Leads
data.description: According to Odaily, SlowMist's Cosine reported on the X platform that the hacker responsible for the Cetus incident has not yet returned the stolen funds. The hacker, who holds over $60 million worth of ETH, has not made any further transfers. Community members have submitted potential leads related to the Cetus hacker, and more details may be disclosed in the future.

data.description: According to BlockBeats, the final figures for the University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index and the one-year inflation rate expectations for May in the United States are scheduled to be released in ten minutes.

data.title: James Wynn Opens Long Bitcoin Position Again
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, James Wynn has just opened a long position in Bitcoin again. The current position size is 46 million US dollars, the opening price is 105,560.2 US dollars, and the liquidation price is 102,550 US dollars.

data.title: James Wynn transferred 344.7 ETH to Binance for sale and then transferred 500,000 USDC to Hyperliquid to open a BTC long order
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, James Wynn collected 344.7 ETH (US$900,000) through multiple addresses in the past half hour and transferred them to Binance for sale, and then withdrew 500,000 USDC from Binance to Hyperliquid to open a BTC long order.
He currently holds more BTC and more PEPE, with positions:
40x more 437 BTC, worth US$46.1 million, opening price US$105,560, liquidation price US$102,713;
10x more 1.01 billion kPEPE, worth US$13.02 million, opening price US$0.0129, liquidation price US$0.0116.
